notes: Card images are treated as mostly static reference assets.
Images should be cached by exact card identity, not by card name alone.
This repository stores image metadata and references only, not binary image files.

externalStorageOptions.fit: Good for low-cost object storage close to edge delivery.
externalStorageOptions.notes: Use signed/public URL strategy and immutable cache-control headers.

externalStorageOptions.fit: Good when CardScanR metadata and auth already rely on Supabase.
externalStorageOptions.notes: Use bucket policies and predictable object paths by canonical image key.

externalStorageOptions.fit: Good for globally cached immutable assets with simple deployment.
externalStorageOptions.notes: Publish-only flow can be automated from validated image manifests.

reasons: Repository size and clone times grow quickly when binary images are committed.
Git diff/review quality degrades for binary-only updates.
Frequent image churn produces noisy commit history and costly CI sync.
Mobile apps already have stronger local disk caching and eviction controls.

recommendation: Keep public repository image delivery URL-first and binary-free. Cache images on-device with bounded size and selective prefetch for saved inventory/recent scans. If server-side binary hosting is needed later, use external object storage/CDN instead of Git blobs.
